The Righteousness of God: We turned to Psalm 7 and brought our longing for vindication before the righteous Judge. Because God tests hearts, opposes the wicked, and appointed judgment through the risen Christ, we were called to repent rather than presume, entrust vengeance to Him, and take refuge in the One whose righteousness saves.
Beauty Through Brokenness: We turned to Psalm 6 and saw that lament gives words to weakness, tears, and the ache of a broken story. Because the Lord hears our weeping and Christ entered our suffering to redeem it, we were invited to bring our pain to God, trust His mercy, and find beauty in His larger story.
Lead Me in Your Righteousness: We turned to Psalm 5 and prayed for the Lord to hear our groaning, oppose wickedness, and lead us in His righteousness. Because we enter His presence only through the abundance of His steadfast love and the righteousness given in Christ, we were called to seek Him morning by morning and rejoice under His favor.
Dwelling in Safety: We turned to Psalm 4 and saw a prayer that moves from distress to quiet confidence in the Lord. Because God has set apart the godly for Himself and answers His people through Christ, we were called to bring our anxiety to Him, turn from false hopes, receive His gladness, and lie down in safety.
Save Me O God: We turned to Psalm 3 and learned to cry out for salvation when enemies, fear, and shame press in. Because Jesus was struck down yet rose again as the reigning King, we can bring our need to God, rest beneath His shield, and trust with confidence that salvation belongs to the Lord.
Christ Our King: We turned to Psalm 2 and saw the nations rage against the Lord and His Anointed while God sets His King on Zion. Because Jesus is the risen Son who rules with justice and mercy, we were called to take refuge in Him, resist rebellion, and live with hope as His kingdom advances and every enemy falls.
Blessed is the Man: We turned to Psalm 1 and saw the blessed life pictured in the righteous man who delights in the law of the Lord. Because Jesus is the truly blessed Man who stands where sinners fail, we were called to turn from wicked counsel, meditate on God's Word, and grow in Christ.
Shaped by the Shema: We turned to Deuteronomy 6:4-9 and heard the call to listen to the Lord, love Him with all we are, and teach His Word diligently. Because He first loved us in Christ and binds us to Himself by grace, we were called to attend to Him, receive His love, and let His truth shape our homes and witness.
I Am the Vine: We turned to John 15 and heard Jesus say, I am the true vine. Because He is the source of life who was cut down for sinners and raised in resurrection power, we were called to abide in Him, depend on His Word and Spirit, receive the Father's pruning, and bear fruit that glorifies God.
